framework.name: NAEP Mathematics Framework
framework.note: NAEP assesses at grades 4, 8, and 12 only. Defines Basic, Proficient, and Advanced achievement levels.

achievement_levels.Basic: Partial mastery of prerequisite knowledge and skills
achievement_levels.Proficient: Solid academic performance demonstrating competency
achievement_levels.Advanced: Superior performance beyond Proficient

standards.description: a) Identify odd and even

achievement_levels.Basic: Partial mastery of prerequisite knowledge and skills fundamental for proficient work at the 4th grade level
achievement_levels.Proficient: Solid academic performance. Students reaching this level have demonstrated competency over challenging subject matter, including subject-matter knowledge, application of such knowledge to real-world situations, and analytical skills appropriate to the subject matter
achievement_levels.Advanced: Superior performance beyond proficient at grade 4. Fourth-grade students performing at this level should apply integrated procedural knowledge and conceptual understanding to complex and non-routine real-world problem solving
